artifact.content_preview:
De : pierre.gagnon@ssc-spc.gc.ca
À : alice.tran@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Cc : marie.lefebvre@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Objet : Demande d'ajout — Modernisation des postes de travail des utilisateurs
Date : 8 mars 2026

Bonjour Alice,

Je vous écris pour soumettre une demande d'ajout de portée au projet
NGIS Phase 3 de la part du gestionnaire de prestation de services.

Dans le cadre de la consolidation des sites B et C, plusieurs employés
des centres de données affectés devront être relocalisés au site A à
Gatineau. Le

extracted_tasks.text: Remplacer 85 ordinateurs portables et 40 postes de travail fixes pour les employés relocalisés au site A.
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: Remplacement de 85 ordinateurs portables... Remplacement de 40 postes de travail fixes... avant le 15 avril 2026

extracted_tasks.text: Examiner la demande d'ajout de portée au projet NGIS Phase 3.
Soumettre la demande d'ajout de portée au comité directeur.
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: la soumettre au comité directeur lors de la prochaine réunion du 10 mars

extracted_tasks.text: Décision de relocaliser les employés des centres de données des sites B et C vers le site A à Gatineau.
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: plusieurs employés des centres de données affectés devront être relocalisés au site A à Gatineau

alerts.detected_ask: Replacement of 85 laptops and 40 desktop workstations for employees relocating from Sites B and C to Site A, estimated at $210,000–$240,000 CAD, to be delivered and configured before April 15, 2026.
alerts.baseline_reference: OUT OF SCOPE: 'End-user device refresh or replacement (laptops, desktops, mobile devices) — addressed under NGIS Phase 4'
alerts.suggested_action: Formally reject the scope addition under the SSC Project Change Management Process and issue a written response to Pierre Gagnon and Marie Lefebvre confirming that end-user device replacement is explicitly out of scope for NGIS Phase 3 and has been assigned to NGIS Phase 4. Do not table this request

llm_calls.content:
```json
{
  "alignment_score": 0.05,
  "drift_type": "contradicts_scope",
  "detected_ask": "Replacement of 85 laptops and 40 desktop workstations for employees relocating from Sites B and C to Site A, estimated at $210,000–$240,000 CAD, to be delivered and configured before April 15, 2026.",
  "baseline_reference": "OUT OF SCOPE: 'End-user device refresh or replacement (laptops, desktops, mobile devices) — addressed under NGIS Phase 4'",
  "suggested_action": "Formally reject the scope addition under the SSC Project Change Management Process and issue a written response to Pierre Gagnon and Marie Lefebvre confirming that end-user device replacement is explicitly out of scope for NGIS Phase 3 and has been assigned to NGIS Phase 4. Do not table this request at the steering committee as a candidate for approval within this project. If the operational need is urgent, redirect the service delivery manager to the NGIS Phase 4 project sponsor or the appropriate procurement authority. Document the request and the rejection decision in the project change log for audit purposes.",
  "reasoning": "The request directly contradicts an explicitly named out-of-scope item in the baseline. The scope baseline unambiguously excludes end-user device refresh or replacement — including laptops and desktops — and assigns that work to NGIS Phase 4. The operational rationale offered (co-location disruption, aging hardware, avoiding a separate project) does not constitute a valid scope justification under the SSC Project Change Management Process; it is a convenience argument that does not alter the formal scope boundary. Additionally, the estimated cost of $210,000–$240,000 CAD would consume approximately 8.75%–10% of the total approved budget of $2,400,000 CAD, a material impact that would require a formal budget amendment approved by the Director General of IT Infrastructure. The proposed delivery date of April 15, 2026 also falls only 15 days before the Go-Live milestone (D-006, April 30, 2026), introducing schedule risk to a constrained critical path. The alignment score of 0.05 reflects that the only marginal connection to the project is the physical relocation context; the substance of the request is entirely outside the approved scope."
}
```

parsed_json.detected_ask: Replacement of 85 laptops and 40 desktop workstations for employees relocating from Sites B and C to Site A, estimated at $210,000–$240,000 CAD, to be delivered and configured before April 15, 2026.
parsed_json.baseline_reference: OUT OF SCOPE: 'End-user device refresh or replacement (laptops, desktops, mobile devices) — addressed under NGIS Phase 4'
parsed_json.suggested_action: Formally reject the scope addition under the SSC Project Change Management Process and issue a written response to Pierre Gagnon and Marie Lefebvre confirming that end-user device replacement is explicitly out of scope for NGIS Phase 3 and has been assigned to NGIS Phase 4. Do not table this request at the steering committee as a candidate for approval within this project. If the operational need is urgent, redirect the service delivery manager to the NGIS Phase 4 project sponsor or the appropriate procurement authority. Document the request and the rejection decision in the project change log for audit purposes.
parsed_json.reasoning: The request directly contradicts an explicitly named out-of-scope item in the baseline. The scope baseline unambiguously excludes end-user device refresh or replacement — including laptops and desktops — and assigns that work to NGIS Phase 4. The operational rationale offered (co-location disruption, aging hardware, avoiding a separate project) does not constitute a valid scope justification under the SSC Project Change Management Process; it is a convenience argument that does not alter the formal scope boundary. Additionally, the estimated cost of $210,000–$240,000 CAD would consume approximately 8.75%–10% of the total approved budget of $2,400,000 CAD, a material impact that would require a formal budget amendment approved by the Director General of IT Infrastructure. The proposed delivery date of April 15, 2026 also falls only 15 days before the Go-Live milestone (D-006, April 30, 2026), introducing schedule risk to a constrained critical path. The alignment score of 0.05 reflects that the only marginal connection to the project is the physical relocation context; the substance of the request is entirely outside the approved scope.
